channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Former EA, Microsoft & Ubisoft Devs Open New Studio, Making ‘Project-S’
item.content:encoded:
A new indie studio from the United Kingdom, chaotic works, is working on a new multiplayer action game called Project-S.

Talking about studios working on games, we have seen claims that NetherRealm Studios is working on the third installment of Injustice, and that Avalanche Studios is also working on a new open-world title. However, today we aren’t looking at these AAA games; instead, a new indie studio is working on its debut multiplayer title.

Chaotic Works Reveals Debut Game, Codenamed Project-S

chaotic works was founded in 2023 by Karl Francis and Lawrence Grabowsi, who have been working on their debut game with limited savings and financial support from UK angel investors. The team now consists of 15 people, including contributors from games like Fallout, Forza Horizon, Splinter Cell, Sniper Elite, and Far Cry, as well as studios such as Microsoft, Ubisoft, Rebellion, EA, Team17, and Unity.

The new studio is focusing on multiplayer titles for PC and consoles. With Project-S, the developers aim to achieve “an unprecedented level of dynamism and hostility that will challenge players both mechanically and socially.” The devs also wish to explore different ways to feature “thrilling adrenaline-fueled action” along with “social dynamics in large, humbling worlds.” Check out their core principles for the upcoming game below:

  • First is to push boundaries to blend action with social dynamics.
  • To create worlds that feature unmatched intensity and unpredictability, which challenge players socially and mechanically.
  • For players to make bonds and rivalries amidst the chaos.
  • The last principle is optimization to ensure that games run on the most basic systems, including portable consoles.

In other news, Mellow’s Indie Launchpad is a new platform that helps ease the burden of indie studios. Additionally, here’s everything announced at April 2026’s ID@Xbox event. What are your thoughts on chaotic works and Project-S? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Take-Two CEO not committing to a price for GTA 6 yet. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Marvel Rivals Season 8 Start Date & New Heroes—Marvel Rivals Season 7.5 End Date, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Marvel Rivals introduces new content quicker than any other hero shooter out there, with Season 8 fast approaching. Expect new heroes to join the roster as the story continues to unfold, with a new villain bound to take centre stage.

That being said, there’s still some time left with Season 7.5, so make sure you take the time to wrap up any loose ends, as you’ll want to give Season 8 your full attention.

For details on when Season 7.5 of Marvel Rivals ends, and Season 8 begins, as well as which characters are supposed to be joining the roster, you’re in the right place.

When Does Marvel Rivals Season 7.5 End? Season 8 Start Date, Explained

As announced in the Season 7.5 roadmap, the current Marvel Rivals season will end on May 15, 2026, with Season 8 kicking off right where it left off.

This makes Season 7.5 a shorter season than usual for Marvel Rivals, with the entire thing wrapped up in just four weeks. Usually, a full Season lasts nine weeks, but this time around it was cut down by a week to run for eight instead.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Claim All Marvel Rivals Path To Doomsday Rewards: The Avengers Event

Sure, that means you have less time to grind Ranked and engage with the season, but on the bright side, it means new content is coming even quicker than usual—hopefully that takes the form of a new Vanguard!

Marvel Rivals Season 8 Start Times

Using other Marvel Rivals seasons as a guideline, it’s reasonable to assume that Season 8 will begin on May 15, 2026, at 3 AM PT.

A new season is always the most exciting time to play Marvel Rivals, with new metas to adjust to and heroes to learn. If you’re ready for a new wave of content, but you’re not from the West Coast, here’s when you can play Marvel Rivals Season 8:

Which Heroes Are Arriving With Marvel Rivals Season 8?

It hasn’t been announced just yet, but judging by the teasers in Season 7.5, there are a few heroes that could be on the cards to join the line-up.

The obvious suggestions are Cyclops and Devil Dinosaur/Moon Girl, as both heroes were teased in the final Gallery Card of Season 7.5. Prior seasons have teased new heroes the same way, and they’ve both been introduced shortly afterwards, so it’d be a surprise if it wasn’t those two.

  • More Insider Gaming: How To Play The Avengers Game Mode In Marvel Rivals—Loki Guide

That being said, there are rumours for other characters, with The Hood, Captain Marvel and Mystique all gaining traction and seemingly feasible based on ongoing lore and previous teasers. Until we get closer to Season 8’s release, though, we’ll have to keep speculating, but as soon as new details are revealed, the article will be promptly updated.

Are you excited for Season 8 of Marvel Rivals? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming release hubs, check out the following:

  • UFC 6
  • Apex Legends Season 29

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Amid $100 Rumors, Take-Two Won’t Commit To GTA 6 Price Yet
item.content:encoded:
Take-Two CEO Strauss Zelnick addressed the oft-discussed rumors about Grand Theft Auto 6’s pricing. While there were no definitive answers, Zelnick provided a look into the mindset the company is approaching the release with.

The comments were made at the IICON conference for gaming executives. Primarily, he emphasized the importance of ensuring that a game’s value exceeds its price point. Additionally, he noted that game pricing has generally decreased over the years, with most games priced between $60 and $70 for over a decade.

Take-Two CEO Won’t Commit to a Price for GTA 6

During his comments at IICON, Strauss Zelnick stated that he believes consumers will pay for the value they get, but as service providers, they have to overload their products with value.

“Consumers pay for the value that you bring to them, and our job is to charge way way way less of the value delivery,” Zelnick said (via IGN). “How you feel about something you buy is the intersection of the thing itself and what you pay for. Consumers need to feel like the thing itself is amazing and the price they were charged was fair for what they got.”

With such intense hype surrounding GTA 6, Zelnick admitted he’s terrified of the idea of measuring the game’s success.

“What we think about is making the most spectacular piece of entertainment on Earth, in history – and it’s a pretty daunting challenge. If we do that, and if we’re of service to our customers, then the upside will take care of itself,” he said.

He also addressed the release date, stating that it would remain the previously announced November date. Learn more about that here. Insider Gaming will continue to monitor the story and report any updates as more information becomes available.

Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Resident Evil Movie’s First Trailer is Live
item.content:encoded:
Zach Cregger’s Resident Evil movie has received its first teaser trailer, building hype for the September 2026 release.

The first trailer for the Resident Evil movie was showcased at CinemaCon, with the creators going all-in on gore. The trailer received a pretty good response from the audience at the event, and now it is finally time for the general public to see it in action. Interested readers can check out the trailer embedded below:

Resident Evil Movie Trailer Provides a Small Glimpse of What’s to Come

The new trailer for the Resident Evil movie begins with Austin Abrams, known for his role in Weapons, searching for a phone in a deserted house located in the middle of a snowy field. After he manages to access the phone, he calls his girlfriend to inform her that “some things have happened” and that he won’t be seeing her anytime soon. From that moment on, the trailer’s tone shifts dramatically, featuring intense action sequences and the zombies Abrams will encounter.

The trailer provides players with a brief look at the various types of zombies featured in the movie. Among them are a massive, blobby monster lurking in the sewers and hordes of zombies attacking from multiple directions, as well as various blood-filled environments. As the trailer progresses, it intensifies, showing Austin running through a narrow lane while zombies fall from rooftops around him like explosive body bombs. This essentially captures the level of horror and gore that director Cregger aims to deliver.

In other news, Resident Evil Requiem developers have provided the first update on the upcoming story DLC. Additionally, the game’s producer has hinted at a different mini-game arriving soon. What are your thoughts on Resident Evil movie’s first trailer? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Star Wars: Galactic Racer’s release date receiving an official announcement. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: NACON Connect Being Held Next Week
item.content:encoded:
NACON is officially holding its Connect event in one week, as of this writing. A new press release confirms that the event will be held on May 7. This comes at a turbulent time for NACON and the video game industry as a whole. The presentation is set to focus on the company’s new projects across its accessories branch and the video games it will publish.

The event was originally scheduled for March but was delayed after the company announced it was filing for emergency insolvency. At the time, the company confidently stated that this would not be the end for them. However, the difficulties have continued to pile on. Recently, it was reported that three of NACON’s studios, Spiders, Kylotonn, and Cyanide, would also be filing for insolvency. Learn more about that at this link.

More on the Upcoming NACON Connect Event

The upcoming NACON Connect Event on May 7 is slated to feature a number of titles, including more details on the recently announced Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish. The presentation will be streamed on the company’s official YouTube and Twitch channels at 1:00 p.m. ET.

“On the program for the evening: announcements of ambitious games, a preview of upcoming innovations for its range of accessories, and exclusive gameplay sequences for anticipated titles such as The Mound, Edge of Memories, Endurance Motorsport Series, and the very recently announced Hunter: The Reckoning – Deathwish,” the company said in a press release.

Insider Gaming will provide full coverage of the event on May 7. For more NACON news, learn about the cancellation of several physical releases the company was planning on.

Are you excited about the upcoming event at all? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
